verdict_reason: The engineering layer preserves pratiṣṭhitā as the recurring technical term and explicitly identifies its cross-verse pattern (2.55, 2.57, 2.58, 2.61, 2.68) as the chapter's defining lexical thread. sarvaśaḥ is preserved as 'on every side, sarvaśaḥ' in the body of the analog. The Sanskrit is not flattened — the engineering layer holds the technical Sanskrit term in active sentences ('The Sanskrit term that returns here, pratiṣṭhitā'). The Advaita / Vishishtadvaita / Gaudiya Vaishnava axis is preserved at the source-pack level (Shankara: nigṛhītāni as withdrawn from sense-objects on the path to ātma-anātma-viveka; Ramanuja: same withdrawal as prerequisite for devotion-firmness; Prabhupada: positive engagement of senses in service rather than mere suppression). The engineering analog scopes the doctrinal axis appropriately to the architecture-of-attention layer, which is tradition-neutral and does not flatten. No conflation. The falsifiability clause ('an engineer who silences genuine production pages has confused the verse with a different one') is the kind of guarded statement that an Indian-philosophy-aware reading produces. Pass.
